links.targetTitle: Connect-on-Swarm data spans four distinct storage layers with different durability
Each storage copy serves a distinct role: local reads, multi-device sync, or self-sovereign recovery
Connect-on-Swarm data spans four distinct storage layers with different durability
Each storage copy serves a distinct role: local reads, multi-device sync, or self-sovereign recovery

global.title: PGlite is the primary store and Swarm /bytes is the durable backup for raw operations
global.content:
Operations have a clear primary/backup relationship across layers:

| Data | PGlite | Swarm /bytes | Swarm feeds | IndexedDB |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Operations (raw) | PRIMARY | BACKUP | — | pending only |
| Document state (computed) | DERIVED | not stored | — | — |
| Doc/drive/user manifests | — | PAYLOAD | POINTER | — |
| Signer key | — | — | — | cache only (re-derivable from wallet) |
| Pending ops buffer | — | — | — | temporary (cleared after flush) |

Key consequences:
- **Document state is derived**, never stored on Swarm — always recomputed from operations on load.
- **Manifests exist in both /bytes and feeds**: the /bytes chunk holds the payload, the feed holds the pointer to the latest version.
- **The signer key is a cache only**, re-derivable from the wallet signature.
- **Pending ops are temporary** — cleared once flushed to Swarm.

This split is intentional: PGlite optimizes hot reads, Swarm optimizes durability, manifests use the feed-as-pointer pattern to make "latest version" lookups cheap.
global.description: Operations are primary in PGlite (hot reads), backed up to Swarm /bytes. Document state is derived from operations and never stored on Swarm.
